name: Qwen 3.5 2B AWQ 4-bit
description: Qwen 3.5 2B AWQ 4-bit community quantisation. Fully multimodal: text, image, video. Supports native tool calls and chain-of-thought reasoning.

notes: vLLM cu132 and torch cu128 coexist via CUDA driver backward-compatible ABI
PaddleOCR removed — image/document understanding handled by native vision
quantization=compressed-tensors auto-detected by vLLM; also set explicitly via --quantization

gpu_memory_utilization=0.98 is the empirical hard ceiling for RTX 5060 8GB. Do NOT raise to 1.0 — causes OOM (7.4/7.53 GiB free vs 7.53 GiB required). VLLM_MEMORY_PROFILER_ESTIMATE_CUDAGRAPHS must NOT be set; it reduces effective utilization to 0.9395 and shrinks KV cache from 68,000 to 61,472 tokens.

block_size = 544 tokens — set by vLLM's mamba page alignment logic (interface.py), NOT by max_model_len. All KV cache sizes are exact multiples of 544: 68,544 = 126 × 544 (max_model_len=9216), 68,000 = 125 × 544 (max_model_len≥~65000). The 1-block (544-token) loss at large max_model_len is caused by vLLM allocating a larger dummy batch during KV cache profiling, consuming slightly more VRAM and leaving 1 fewer block.

max_model_len auto-calibration: vllm_launcher.py reads the actual KV cache from vllm_api.log and computes optimal = (kv_cache_tokens // block_size) * block_size = kv_cache_tokens (already block-aligned). This creates a stable fixed point: once max_model_len == kv_cache_tokens, the same VRAM profiling overhead produces the same kv_cache_tokens on the next boot → no further updates. The floor is 65536. The calibrated value is written back to this JSON automatically.
resource_tracker: 1 leaked semaphore at shutdown — vLLM-internal multiprocessing cleanup artefact, not actionable from application code. Expected and harmless.
Overriding num_gpu_blocks=0 with num_gpu_blocks_override=512 — vLLM internal calibration step, not an error.
